description: baseline
Increase WARMDOWN_RATIO from 0.5 to 0.75 for more aggressive LR cooldown over 75% of training time instead of 50%, allowing more steps at lower learning rates near convergence
Increase weight decay on embedding layers (wte, lm_head, value_embeds) from 0.001 to 0.003
Replace linear LR warmdown with cosine annealing for smoother learning rate decay
Add label smoothing (0.1) to cross-entropy loss to improve generalization and reduce model overconfidence
Add 0.1 dropout to residual connections in transformer blocks to reduce overfitting
Increase z-loss coefficient from 1e-4 to 1e-3 for stronger logit regularization
Add 5% linear LR warmup at the start of training to stabilize early optimization
Reduce logits softcap from 12 to 8 for stronger regularization
Add 0.1 dropout to token embeddings (wte) to reduce overfitting
Change RoPE base frequency from 50000 to 10000 for improved long-range dependency modeling
Add stochastic depth (layer dropout) with 10% drop probability to transformer blocks for regularization
Increase weight decay on embedding layers (wte, lm_head, value_embeds) from 0.001 to 0.005 for stronger regularization
Change MLP activation from ReLU^2 to GELU for smoother gradients and better optimization
Change attention window pattern from SSSL to SSSS to use shorter windows in all layers except last, increasing training throughput for more steps in the fixed time budget
Initialize x0_lambdas to 0.2 instead of 0.1 for stronger deep residual skip connections from input
Initialize resid_lambdas to 0.9 instead of 1.0 to reduce main residual path weight and complement strong x0 skip connections
Increase logits softcap from 12 to 20 to allow higher logit magnitudes before tanh saturation
Share value embeddings across all layers instead of separate per-layer embeddings
Align value embeddings Adam betas with regular embeddings (0.8, 0.95) instead of (0.9, 0.999) for potentially faster adaptation of value residual connections
Initialize resid_lambdas to 0.9 and x0_lambdas to 0.2 to combine deep residual skip connections with reduced main path weight
Initialize resid_lambdas to 0.8 for stronger residual path reduction
Reduce weight decay from 0.2 to 0.1 for less regularization on matrix parameters (Muon), potentially improving fitting in the limited training time.

Initialize resid_lambdas with linear decay from 1.0 to 0.6 across layers instead of constant 1.0, providing stronger residual paths in early layers and weaker in deeper layers while maintaining mean around 0.8 (the current best constant value) [return compiled_fn(runtime_args)
Remove z-loss regularization by zeroing its coefficient to reduce gradient noise and potential interference during the short 5-minute training run
Initialize resid_lambdas to 0.75 for stronger residual path reduction
Change attention window pattern from SSSL to LLSS to use more long-range attention layers (50% instead of 25%), trading throughput for better long-range dependency modeling capacity
Replace MLP activation from ReLU^2 to SiLU (Swish) for improved gradient flow and expressivity
Switch from multi-head attention to grouped-query attention (GQA) by reducing n_kv_head from 6 to 2, cutting KV cache memory bandwidth and FLOPs to increase training throughput (more steps in 5 min)
Add bias terms to all linear layers in transformer blocks (attention Q/K/V/proj and MLP fc/proj) to improve optimization capacity [Traceback (most recent call last):
Remove internal residual connection in MLP to standardize transformer block structure (avoid double addition of normalized input)
Initialize x0_lambdas to 0.0 instead of 0.1 to disable deep residual skip connections initially, forcing the model to learn them from scratch
Combine best residual config: init resid_lambdas to 0.8 (current best) and x0_lambdas to 0.2 (near-miss) for stronger deep skip connections with reduced main path

Remove weight decay on all embedding parameters (wte, lm_head, value_embeds) to reduce regularization on high-dimensional sparse update parameters, potentially improving convergence in the limited 5-minute training window.
Use constant gradient clipping at max_norm=1.0 instead of adaptive cosine schedule decaying to 0.3, providing consistent gradient scaling throughout training
Reduce z-loss coefficient from 1e-4 to 5e-5 for less aggressive logit regularization

Combine best residual initialization (resid_lambda=0.8, x0_lambda=0.2) with zero weight decay on all embedding parameters (wte, lm_head, value_embeds) to reduce regularization on high-dimensional sparse updates while maintaining strong deep skip connections
Set final LR fraction to 0.1 to prevent learning rate from decaying to exactly zero, allowing small adaptive updates until the end of the short training window
Change gradient clipping from adaptive cosine decay (1.0 to 0.3) to linear decay (1.0 to 0.5) for less aggressive clipping at the end of training
Apply temperature scaling of 0.8 to logits before softcap to sharpen prediction distribution and accelerate convergence in the limited training window
Apply attention temperature scaling by multiplying queries by 1.2 after QK normalization to sharpen attention distribution and accelerate convergence

Initialize value embeddings to zero instead of uniform random to reduce initial noise in the value residual path, allowing the model to gradually learn value embeddings from scratch while starting with standard attention values
Remove weight decay cosine decay schedule to keep weight decay constant at 0.2 throughout training instead of decaying to 0.02, maintaining stronger regularization for the full 5-minute window
Combine best residual initialization (resid_lambdas=0.8, x0_lambdas=0.2) with temperature scaling of 0.8 on logits before softcap to sharpen predictions while maintaining strong deep skip connections
Replace MLP activation with SwiGLU (SiLU-gated linear unit) using 8/3*d_model hidden dim to maintain params, improve gradient flow
Combine best residual initialization (resid_lambdas=0.8, x0_lambdas=0.2) with logits temperature scaling (T=0.8) and small final LR fraction (0.01) to allow micro-updates at training end

Double the learning rate for value embeddings to accelerate their adaptation in the limited 5-minute training window, as they are high-dimensional sparse parameters that may benefit from faster updates.

FAILED TO APPLY: Apply cosine decay to AdamW weight decay (embeddings, lm_head, value_embeds, scalars) proportionally to Muon decay, reducing regularization on sparse high-dim parameters over time for finer late-training convergence

Initialize attention and MLP output projections (c_proj) with uniform random values instead of zeros to provide non-zero initial signal through residual paths, potentially accelerating early training dynamics
Combine the winning double learning rate for value embeddings with temperature scaling of 0.8 on logits before softcap to sharpen the output distribution while accelerating value embedding adaptation

Initialize VE gate weights to -2.0 instead of 0.0 to start value residual contributions at ~24% (sigmoid(-2)*2) and allow gradual ramp-up during training, potentially stabilizing early optimization of high-dimensional value embeddings
Reduce Muon orthogonalization steps from 5 to 4 to decrease per-step compute cost, allowing more training steps within the fixed 5-minute time budget.
Apply exponential moving average (EMA) to model weights with decay 0.99 during training and use EMA weights for final evaluation to improve generalization.

Combine double learning rate for value embeddings with residual initialization (resid_lambdas=0.8, x0_lambdas=0.2) to accelerate value embedding adaptation while maintaining strong deep skip connections
Remove weight decay specifically from value embeddings (set to 0.0) while keeping weight decay for other embeddings, reducing regularization on high-dimensional sparse value residual parameters

Keep residual connection scalars (resid_lambdas and x0_lambdas) at constant learning rate throughout training by skipping LR decay for their parameter groups, allowing continuous adaptation of deep skip connection weights
Reduce sliding window size from half to quarter context (//4 instead of //2) to increase training throughput for more steps in the 5-minute budget
Reduce gradient clipping aggressiveness by raising minimum clip norm from 0.3 to 0.7 to allow larger updates late in training
Increase ve_gate_channels from 32 to full embedding dimension (config.n_embd) to give value residual gate access to complete input information for better value embedding utilization
Set final LR fraction to 0.01 to allow micro-updates at training end instead of decaying to exactly zero
Combine double learning rate for value embeddings with zero weight decay on value embeddings to reduce regularization on high-dimensional sparse parameters while accelerating their adaptation

Apply gradient clipping only to Muon (matrix) parameters, allowing AdamW parameters (embeddings, scalars) to use unclipped gradients for potentially faster adaptation of high-dimensional sparse parameters
Cast all model parameters to bfloat16 after initialization to reduce memory bandwidth pressure and increase training throughput, allowing more optimization steps within the fixed 5-minute time budget
Combine double learning rate for value embeddings with 33% higher LR for x0 residual scalars (4.0x vs 3.0x) to accelerate adaptation of both value residuals and deep skip connections
Combine double learning rate for value embeddings with increased Muon weight decay (0.3) for stronger regularization on matrix parameters
Increase learning rate for residual path scalars (resid_lambdas) from 0.025 to 0.1 to accelerate adaptation of main residual weights toward optimal values during the short training window

Keep AdamW parameters (embeddings, value embeddings, lm_head, and scalars) at constant full learning rate without warmdown, while allowing Muon matrix parameters to follow the cosine learning rate schedule.
